extra.message: Unsanitized input from cookies flows into pickle.loads, where it is used to deserialize an object. This may result in an Unsafe Deserialization vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from an uploaded file flows into yaml.load, where it is used to deserialize an object. This may result in an Unsafe Deserialization vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into open, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to read arbitrary files.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into open, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to read arbitrary files.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into eval, where it is executed as Python code. This may result in a Code Injection vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into eval, where it is executed as Python code. This may result in a Code Injection vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into django.contrib.auth.login.objects.raw, where it is used in an SQL query. This may result in an SQL Injection vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into introduction.models.sql_lab_table.objects.raw, where it is used in an SQL query. This may result in an SQL Injection vulnerability.
A hardcoded string value is used as a cipher key in jwt.decode. Generate the value with a cryptographically strong random number generator instead.
A hardcoded string value is used as a cipher key in jwt.decode. Generate the value with a cryptographically strong random number generator instead.
Avoid hardcoding values that are meant to be secret. Found a hardcoded string used in here.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into subprocess.Popen, where it is used as a shell command. This may result in a Command Injection vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from a web form flows into pickle.loads, where it is used to deserialize an object. This may result in an Unsafe Deserialization vulnerability.
Avoid hardcoding values that are meant to be secret. Found a hardcoded string used in here.

Unsanitized user input from the HTTP request body flows into xml.dom.pulldom.parseString, with external entities being enabled. This introduces the risk of XML External Entity Injection (XXE) attacks.
CSRF protection is disabled by django.views.decorators.csrf.csrf_exempt. This allows the attackers to execute requests on a user's behalf.

Unsanitized input from data from a remote resource flows into innerHTML, where it is used to dynamically construct the HTML page on client side. This may result in a DOM Based Cross-Site Scripting attack (DOMXSS).
Running the application in debug mode (debug flag is set to True in run) is a security risk if the application is accessible by untrusted parties.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into requests.get, where it is used as an URL to perform a request. This may result in a Server Side Request Forgery vulnerability.
